title: gomphrena paranensis subsp. paranensis
intro: Gomphrena paranensis subsp. paranensis, also known as "earth apple" or "Amazonian gomphrena," is a plant species within the Amaranthaceae family, commonly found in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Gomphrena paranensis subsp. paranensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
